items.title: Housing prices with collinear square footage measures
items.scenario: A real-estate model includes living_area_sqft, lot_sqft, and rooms—all correlated. OLS assigns large opposing coefficients that swing with small data changes; Ridge stabilizes them by shrinking jointly.
items.explanation: Ridge's L2 penalty keeps correlated predictors in the model with moderated weights instead of amplifying noise through (XᵀX)⁻¹ instability.

items.title: Gene expression with thousands of features
items.scenario: A medical study has 20,000 gene measurements but only 80 patients. OLS is ill-posed; Lasso selects a handful of genes with non-zero coefficients for a sparse prognostic signature.
items.explanation: L1 sparsity is valuable when p ≫ n: Lasso zeros most coefficients, yielding an interpretable short list of predictors.

items.title: Sales forecasting baseline before deep models
items.scenario: A retailer builds a weekly revenue forecast from 50 engineered tabular features. A cross-validated Ridge pipeline with standardized inputs beats unconstrained linear regression on holdout weeks.
items.explanation: Tuning λ on validation MSE is standard practice: moderate regularization reduces variance without the complexity of a neural net.
